name: Encañizadas Trail
name_original: Las Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar — Circular por el Sendero de las Encañizadas (PR-MU 65)
region: Salinas Y Arenales De San Pedro Del Pinatar
description: Hike the 7.7-mile circular Sendero de las Encañizadas (PR-MU 65) through salt flats, beaches, and bird-filled wetlands in San Pedro del Pina
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Salinas Y Arenales De San Pedro Del Pinatar traverse a unique landscape characterized by diverse ecosystems, including salt flats, sand dunes, and pine forests. This Regional Park, recognized for its ecological importance, offers predominantly flat terrain with minimal elevation changes, making it ideal for accessible walks. Hikers can explore a variety of natural features, from the vibrant salt marshes to tranquil beaches and reed beds. The area provides a gentle environment for outdoor activities, suitable for all ages. Best family friendly hiking trails in Salinas Y Arenales De San Pedro Del Pinatar The most popular family friendly hiking route is Encañizadas Trail, a 7.7 miles (12.5 km) moderate trail that takes about 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route showcases reed beds, salt ponds, pine forests, dunes, and traditional fishing methods. Another top favourite among local hikers is Coterillo Lagoon – Torre Derribada Beach loop from ZEPA Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar, an easy 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This trail leads around Coterillo Pond, offering views of diverse flora and fauna. Local hikers also love the La Playuela Beach – La Llana Beach loop from ZEPA Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading along picturesque beaches, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Hiking in Salinas Y Arenales De San Pedro Del Pinatar is defined by salt flats, sand dunes, and coastal paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, with many easy and moderate routes. The routes in Salinas Y Arenales De San Pedro Del Pinatar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 6000 hikers have used komoot to explore Salinas Y Arenales De San Pedro Del Pinatar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar?
A: There are over 20 family-friendly hiking trails in Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar, with the vast majority being easy and suitable for all ages. These routes offer a wonderful way to explore the region's unique natural beauty.

Q: What kind of terrain can we expect on family hikes in this region?
A: The trails in Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar are generally flat and easily accessible, making them ideal for families. You'll walk through diverse landscapes including salt flats, pine forests, reed beds, and sand dunes, often on well-marked paths.

Q: Are there circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, several excellent circular routes are perfect for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/517681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coterillo Lagoon – Torre Derribada Beach loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a pleasant walk around the lagoon and along the beach. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14629726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Playuela Beach – La Llana Beach loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you along picturesque coastal stretches.

Q: Can we bring our dog on these trails?
A: Many trails within the Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar Regional Park are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially in areas designated for bird protection. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

Q: What interesting natural features or landmarks can we see along the family-friendly hiking trails?
A: You can discover a variety of fascinating sights. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt Flats and Sand Dunes of San Pedro del Pinatar&lt;/a&gt; are a prominent feature, offering unique landscapes. You might also spot the historic Molino Quintín windmill. Many trails lead to or pass by the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3920897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mud Baths at Villananitos Beach&lt;/a&gt;, a popular attraction.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching with children?
A: Absolutely! The park is a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA) and is fantastic for birdwatching. Flamingos are a spectacular sight, especially in late June, but you can also spot gulls, herons, and other waterfowl. There's even a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4296153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bird Observation Deck&lt;/a&gt; in the Salinas de San Pedro Regional Park, perfect for families.

Q: Are the trails suitable for strollers or very young children?
A: Many of the trails are well-suited for strollers and very young children due to their flat and accessible nature. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17271716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rotonda de los Flamencos – Coterillo Lagoon loop&lt;/a&gt; are generally wide and even, making them comfortable for families with pushchairs.

Q: When is the best time of year to hike with family in this region?
A: Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are generally the most pleasant times for family hikes. The weather is milder, and you avoid the intense heat of summer. These seasons also offer excellent opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: Where can we park when visiting with family?
A: Parking is generally available at various access points to the Regional Park. The Visitor Center at the entrance often has parking facilities, and you'll find designated parking areas near popular trailheads, such as those leading to the beaches or the mud baths.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique natural landscapes, the abundance of birdlife (especially flamingos), and the ease of the trails, making them perfect for a relaxed family outing. The opportunity to combine a walk with a visit to the mud baths is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Can we combine hiking with other activities like the mud baths?
A: Yes, absolutely! Many family-friendly trails lead directly to or are very close to the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3920897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mud Baths at Villananitos Beach&lt;/a&gt;. It's a popular and unique experience to enjoy a therapeutic mud bath after a refreshing walk, making for a memorable family day out.

Q: Is there a visitor center or information point for families?
A: Yes, there is a Visitor Center located at the entrance of the Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar Regional Park. It provides valuable information on the park's environmental assets, available routes, and can offer guidance on the best trails for families.
tips:
These mills next to the salt flats and the sea make a wonderful landscape!
Formerly used to take water from the sea and dump it on the surface of the salt flats, today they are part of the picturesque landscape of the Salinas de San Pedro del Pinatar.

Just as the name indicates, a beautiful but flat beach that divides the Salinas de San Pedro from the Mediterranean Sea. Like the other beaches in this sector, they do not have much shelter from the wind and on those windy days it can be uncomfortable, so I recommend going on a calm day.
Nice viewpoint over the evaporation basins for the extraction of salt from the seawater and the still working salt factory.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike The Salinas and Arenales of San Pedro del Pinatar — Circular via the Sendero de las Encañizadas (PR-MU 65)?
faqs.answer: This circular route typically takes around 3 hours and 8 minutes of active walking to complete. While the terrain is mostly flat, its length of approximately 12.4 kilometers contributes to the overall duration.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty and terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is largely flat with minimal elevation changes, making it accessible for most fitness levels. Komoot rates it as moderate, primarily due to its length. You'll encounter diverse terrain, including well-marked paths, active salt flats, and sandy sections, particularly from the port to Punta de Algas. It's marked with white and yellow PR blazes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll discover a rich variety of sights. Highlights include the historic La Calcetera Windmill, the beautiful La Llana Beach, and the Viewpoint of Salinas de San Pedro. The trail also passes through active salt flats with salt mountains, offers views of both the Mar Menor and Mediterranean Sea, and goes by the ancient "Encañizadas" fishing system and therapeutic mud baths.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero de las Encañizadas (PR-MU 65) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the sensitive ecosystem of the Regional Park and the presence of diverse birdlife. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your dog, as water sources are limited.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is during the cooler months, from autumn through spring. It's advisable to avoid the peak summer months (June to September) due to high temperatures and a lack of shade and water sources along the route, which can make the experience uncomfortable.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends at the "Las Salinas" Visitor Center, which is a convenient point for information and facilities. Parking is usually available near the visitor center, providing easy access to the beginning of the circular route.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Parque Regional de las Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar?
faqs.answer: No, there is no entrance fee or permit required to hike this trail within the Parque Regional de las Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar. The park is freely accessible to the public, allowing visitors to enjoy its natural beauty and unique ecosystems.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and flora can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The park is an internationally important wetland and a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA). You'll have excellent opportunities for birdwatching, with sightings of flamingos, avocets, and royal herons, especially near the salt pans. The unique flora adapted to saline and dune environments, such as Posidonia oceanica on the beaches, is also notable.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route largely follows the official Sendero de las Encañizadas (99% of the route) and also intersects with significant portions of the Transmurciana 03 (89%) and the Sendero del Mediterráneo - Murcia (19%). You'll also find smaller sections overlapping with Camino Quintin and Transmurciana 02.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or public transport nearby?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself has limited amenities, the town of San Pedro del Pinatar is nearby and offers cafes, restaurants, and other services. Public transport options to the vicinity of the park and visitor center are available, though it's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a comfortable hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and exposure, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, especially outside of cooler months, as there are no water sources along the route. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is essential due to minimal shade. Comfortable walking shoes suitable for flat and sandy terrain are also advised.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of Salinas de San Pedro
